narrative:
At a current price of $234.27 per share and trailing FCF of $139.5B, the reverse DCF asks: what 10-year FCF growth rate would justify this price under various assumptions?

Under the central assumptions (10% discount rate, 20× terminal P/FCF), the market is pricing in approximately 3.5% annual FCF growth for 10 years.
For comparison, actual FCF CAGR has been 16.1% over the past 5 years and 44.0% over the past 3 years.
The implied growth is below the historical pace — the market may be pricing in a deceleration or slowdown.

Read the sensitivity grid below to see how this implied growth shifts under different discount rates and terminal multiples. A value-investing skeptic would ask whether the highest-implied-growth cells in the grid are achievable, and whether the assumptions that produce a more attractive picture are themselves reasonable.
